Idiomatic Expressions

  • 燕趙間 - Refers to the region between the states of Yan and Zhao, historically known for its rugged terrain and fierce people.
  • 鄉農 - Refers to a farmer or rural dweller, often used to describe someone from the countryside.
  • 中年續娶 - Means 'to remarry in middle age,' indicating a second marriage later in life.
  • 婦年十五 - Means 'the wife was fifteen years old,' highlighting her youth.
  • 性好淫 - Means 'having a tendency towards promiscuity,' which is a strong and direct characterization.
  • 其夫酒徒也 - Means 'his husband was a drunkard,' indicating a habitual drinker.
  • 時往時還 - Means 'coming and going irregularly,' suggesting an unpredictable pattern.
  • 更历十六年 - Means 'after sixteen years,' emphasizing the passage of time.
  • 子已长成 - Means 'the son has grown up,' indicating maturity.
  • 贫无二室 - Means 'poor and without a separate room,' highlighting their financial hardship.
  • 母子同卧起 - Means 'mother and son slept and woke together,' indicating close living conditions.
  • 强与子奸 - Means 'forced herself on the son,' a highly sensitive and morally charged phrase.
  • 交恶其夫 - Means 'hated her husband,' indicating a strained relationship.
  • 醉而毙之 - Means 'killed him in a drunken state,' a direct and graphic description.
  • 分其尸埋炕内 - Means 'divided his body and buried it in the kang (heating stove),' a gruesome detail.
  • 值秋涝 - Means 'during the autumn floods,' setting the scene.
  • 随流民出关 - Means 'followed the refugees out of the pass,' indicating a mass movement.
  • 阅三四年 - Means 'after three or four years,' indicating a period of time.
  • 以勤俭积东钱百十贯 - Means 'accumulated a hundred or so strings of copper coins through diligence and frugality,' indicating financial success.
  • 适市有杂货肆 - Means 'happened to be in a market with a miscellaneous goods store,' setting the scene.
  • 主西人 - Means 'the owner was a Westerner,' indicating a foreigner.
  • 欲贱售还乡 - Means 'wanted to sell cheaply and return home,' indicating a desire to leave.
  • 俾奸生子附学读书 - Means 'allowed the illegitimate son to attend school and study,' indicating a change in attitude.
  • 又阅五六年 - Means 'after another five or six years,' indicating a longer period of time.
  • 业日隆盛 - Means 'business grew increasingly prosperous,' indicating success.
  • 内歉外丰 - Means 'internal shortage and external abundance,' indicating economic conditions.
  • 胞兄亦逃出口 - Means 'her brother also escaped to the frontier,' indicating a personal connection.
  • 有老人指令投乡亲 - Means 'an old man instructed him to seek out his fellow villagers,' indicating guidance.
  • 引至肆 - Means 'led him to the store,' indicating a direct action.
  • 母子相见愕然 - Means 'mother and son met in surprise,' indicating a shocked reaction.
  • 喜出意外 - Means 'joyfully surprised,' indicating a positive emotional response.
  • 问妹夫何在 - Means 'asked where her husband was,' indicating a query.
  • 则支离其词 - Means 'but evaded the question,' indicating a lack of honesty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 燕趙間 - Refers to the historical region between the states of Yan and Zhao, known for its rugged terrain and fierce people.
  • 鄉農 - Refers to a farmer or rural dweller, often used to describe someone from the countryside.
  • 中年續娶 - Indicates a second marriage later in life, which was not uncommon in traditional Chinese society.
  • 婦年十五 - Highlights the youth of the wife, which is unusual and raises questions about the marriage.
  • 性好淫 - Refers to a tendency towards promiscuity, which is a strong and direct characterization.
  • 其夫酒徒也 - Indicates a habitual drinker, which was a common social issue in traditional Chinese society.
  • 时往时还 - Suggests an unpredictable pattern of behavior, which adds to the complexity of the narrative.
  • 更历十六年 - Emphasizes the passage of time, which is significant in the context of the story.
  • 子已长成 - Indicates the son's maturity, which is important for the subsequent events.
  • 贫无二室 - Highlights the financial hardship, which is a common theme in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 母子同卧起 - Indicates close living conditions, which adds to the intensity of the situation.
  • 强与子奸 - A highly sensitive and morally charged phrase, which is a serious violation of social norms.
  • 交恶其夫 - Indicates a strained relationship, which is a key element of the story.
  • 醉而毙之 - A direct and graphic description of a violent act, which is a critical point in the narrative.
  • 分其尸埋炕内 - A gruesome detail, which adds to the horror of the situation.
  • 值秋涝 - Sets the scene during the autumn floods, which is a common natural disaster in the region.
  • 随流民出关 - Indicates a mass movement of refugees, which reflects historical events.
  • 阅三四年 - Indicates a period of time, which is important for the development of the story.
  • 以勤俭积东钱百十贯 - Indicates financial success through diligence and frugality, which is a positive outcome.
  • 适市有杂货肆 - Sets the scene in a market with a miscellaneous goods store, which is a common setting in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 主西人 - Indicates a foreigner, which adds an element of cultural diversity.
  • 欲贱售还乡 - Indicates a desire to leave, which is a common theme in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 俾奸生子附学读书 - Indicates a change in attitude, which is a significant development in the story.
  • 又阅五六年 - Indicates a longer period of time, which is important for the development of the story.
  • 业日隆盛 - Indicates business success, which is a positive outcome.
  • 内歉外丰 - Indicates economic conditions, which are important for the context of the story.
  • 胞兄亦逃出口 - Indicates a personal connection, which adds depth to the narrative.
  • 有老人指令投乡亲 - Indicates guidance, which is a common theme in traditional Chinese literature.
  • 引至肆 - Indicates a direct action, which is a key element of the story.
  • 母子相见愕然 - Indicates a shocked reaction, which is a significant emotional moment.
  • 喜出意外 - Indicates a positive emotional response, which is a key element of the story.
  • 问妹夫何在 - Indicates a query, which is a key element of the story.
  • 则支离其词 - Indicates a lack of honesty, which is a critical point in the narrative.

[630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未几 - Means 'shortly after' or 'in a little while,' indicating a brief passage of time.
  • 见二子自塾归 - Literally 'saw two sons returning from the school,' where '塾' refers to a private school or academy.
  • 母其妹而父其甥 - A confusing phrase that suggests a reversal of familial roles, where the mother is treated as the sister and the father as the uncle. This is likely a metaphorical or symbolic statement rather than a literal description.
  • 虽不解其故，亦虑不及此也 - Means 'even though they did not understand the reason, they did not consider it either,' indicating a lack of comprehension or concern.
  • 妇不能不留兄宿 - Means 'the wife could not help but keep the brother to stay,' implying a sense of obligation or necessity.
  • 惧泄机关 - Means 'fearing the revelation of secrets' or 'fearing the exposure of the mechanism,' suggesting a concern about uncovering hidden truths.
  • 明日赠千钱遣之 - Means 'the next day, she gave him a thousand coins and sent him away,' indicating a gesture of compensation or dismissal.
  • 此地官不准留外人 - Means 'the local officials do not allow outsiders to stay,' referring to a rule or regulation.
  • 毋累我也 - Means 'do not trouble me,' a polite request to avoid causing inconvenience.
  • 其兄不肯行，则挥逐之 - Means 'when the brother refused to leave, she hit him and drove him away,' indicating a forceful action.
  • 复遇前老人曰 - Means 'later, she met the previous old man and said,' introducing a new character or dialogue.
  • 既遇至亲，何不多留几日 - Means 'since you have encountered a close relative, why not stay for a few more days,' a question expressing surprise or confusion.
  • 其兄以妹母子无情告 - Means 'the brother reported that his sister and her son were heartless,' indicating a complaint or accusation.
  • 是夫妇也，街坊咸知 - Means 'they are a married couple, and everyone in the neighborhood knows,' providing context about the relationship.
  • 生子时，我妻为之收产 - Means 'when the child was born, my wife helped with the delivery,' referring to a personal experience.
  • 何得谓母子 - Means 'how can they be called mother and son,' questioning the legitimacy of the relationship.
  • 其兄惊悟报官 - Means 'the brother was shocked and reported to the authorities,' indicating a realization and action.
  • 拘讯得实 - Means 'after interrogation, the truth was revealed,' referring to an investigation.
  • 光天化日之下，岂可容此恶兽 - Means 'under the bright sun, how can such a beast be tolerated,' a strong condemnation of the situation.
  • 然无律可援，亦不敢上污圣聪 - Means 'however, there is no law to support this, and I dare not tarnish the wisdom of the emperor,' expressing a legal and moral dilemma.
  • 缚母子四人于狱，绝其食毙之 - Means 'they tied the mother and her three children in prison and starved them to death,' describing a harsh punishment.
  • 焚尸于野，投骨于河 - Means 'they burned the bodies in the wild and threw the bones into the river,' describing the disposal of the remains.
  • 以家业断给其兄作奖赏完结 - Means 'they divided the family property and gave it to the brother as a reward to conclude the matter,' indicating a resolution.
  • 或曰 - Means 'someone said,' introducing a comment or opinion.
  • 若使上谳 - Means 'if it were to be judged by a higher court,' referring to a hypothetical scenario.
  • 母子佥拟凌迟 - Means 'the mother and son were both sentenced to be executed by slicing,' describing a severe punishment.
  • 其二子杀之乎？放之乎？ - Means 'should the two sons be killed? Or released?' presenting a dilemma.
  • 穷于拟议矣 - Means 'it is difficult to decide,' indicating a lack of clear resolution.
  • 予曰 - Means 'I say,' introducing the speaker's response.
  • 应请比照叛逆案内犯该凌迟者 - Means 'it should be requested to compare with cases of rebellion that were sentenced to be executed by slicing,' suggesting a legal precedent.
  • 其子孙解交内务府，阉割发塞外，给官兵为奴 - Means 'their descendants should be handed over to the Imperial Household Department, castrated, and sent to the frontier to serve as slaves to the soldiers,' describing a harsh punishment.
  • 既不戕无知之命，又不留悖逆之裔 - Means 'neither harming innocent lives nor leaving behind traitorous offspring,' justifying the punishment.
  • 老法家以为然否？ - Means 'do the old legal scholars agree with this?' seeking validation from legal experts.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 塾 - Refers to a private school or academy, reflecting the educational system of the time.
  • 凌迟 - A form of execution by slicing, one of the most brutal punishments in Chinese history.
  • 内务府 - The Imperial Household Department, a central administrative body in the Qing dynasty responsible for managing the imperial household.
  • 叛逆 - Refers to treason or rebellion, a serious crime punishable by severe penalties.
  • 光天化日之下 - A phrase meaning 'under the bright sun,' often used to emphasize the public nature of an event or action.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase is ambiguous and likely metaphorical, suggesting a reversal of familial roles.
  • This phrase is somewhat ambiguous, as '机关' can mean 'mechanism' or 'secret,' depending on the context.

[631]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有小家子年十二 - Refers to a child from a poor family, twelve years old.
  • 父母以疫相继亡 - Parents died one after another due to an epidemic.
  • 胞伯父母年老无子，嗣为子 - Uncle and aunt, who are close relatives, adopted the child as their son.
  • 甚爱之 - Very much loved.
  • 老夫妇皆好静 - Enjoy peace and quiet.
  • 子甚顽劣 - Mischievous and unruly.
  • 另居一室 - Live separately in one room.
  • 过午不起 - Did not get up past noon.
  • 呼之不应 - Called to but did not respond.
  • 穴窗而窥 - Peep through a hole in the window.
  • 悬梁自尽矣 - Hanged himself and committed suicide.
  • 夫妇惊泣 - Were shocked and wept.
  • 呼邻里掇门入室 - Called the neighbors to remove the door and enter the room.
  • 室中仅有土炕，无椅桌之类 - The room had only a simple earth bed, no chairs or tables.
  • 自炕至所悬之梁，相距九尺余 - The distance from the bed to the hanging beam was more than nine feet.
  • 童子何能跃而上 - How could the child jump up?
  • 奇矣 - Strange or unusual.
  • 邻人取梯解尸下 - Neighbors took a ladder to lower the body.
  • 系四尺余裤带自缢者 - Tied a belt more than four feet long to hang himself.
  • 梁木围三尺 - The beam had a circumference of three feet.
  • 项上仅有尺余 - Only more than a foot long on the neck.
  • 何以先给扣而后入颈 - How could it first be tied around the neck and then go into the neck?
  • 则更奇矣 - Even more strange.
  • 里胥报官 - Village official reported to the authorities.
  • 其伯拦验，未经通报 - Uncle stopped to examine without reporting.
  • 因案属奇异，官不得不往验尸身 - Because the case was unusual, the official had to come to examine the body.
  • 脑后八字不交，确系自缢，并无他故 - The back of the neck showed an 'eight' shape without crossing, definitely self-hanging, no other cause.
  • 研鞠其子 - Investigated the child.
  • 生前为伯父母所钟爱，众证确凿 - Loved by adoptive parents before death, evidence was clear.
  • 讯其家业，则织布营生，并无恒产 - Inquired about family's livelihood, made a living by weaving, had no permanent property.
  • 可知非谋财故杀 - It can be seen, not a murder for money.
  • 童子有何哀痛迫切，竟至忿激轻生耶 - What sorrow or urgency, led him to commit suicide out of anger?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 小家子 - Refers to children from poor families.
  • 胞伯父母 - Refers to the child's uncle and aunt, who are close relatives.
  • 悬梁自尽 - A common method of suicide in ancient China, involving hanging oneself.
  • 土炕 - A traditional Chinese bed made of earth, commonly used in rural areas.
  • 里胥 - A local village official responsible for minor administrative tasks.
  • 八字不交 - A forensic term indicating the position of the neck after hanging, often used to determine the cause of death.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sequence introduces the child, his parents' death, and his adoption by his uncle and aunt.
  • This sentence describes the lifestyle of the adoptive parents and the child's behavior.
  • This sequence describes the discovery of the child's suicide.
  • This part details the examination of the room and the unusual nature of the suicide.
  • This part further describes the physical details of the suicide and the strangeness of the situation.
  • This part explains the official response to the unusual case.
  • This part concludes with the investigation and the question of why the child committed suicide.

[632]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无子 - Literally 'no son,' but in this context, it implies the couple has no biological male heir.
  • 以姊女名银姑者为继女 - This phrase indicates that the couple adopted their sister's daughter, named Yin Gu, as their stepdaughter.
  • 过度 - This term is ambiguous. It could mean 'excessive' or 'too much,' but in this context, it likely refers to living together in a way that is inappropriate or too intimate.
  • 卧炕不起 - Literally 'lying on the kang (a type of bed) unable to get up,' indicating the woman is bedridden due to illness.
  • 担水者送水至 - This phrase describes a water carrier delivering water, but the context suggests that the household is being left unattended.
  • 纠集邻里宗亲 - Literally 'gathered neighbors and relatives,' indicating a community response to the situation.
  • 肩门入视 - Literally 'shouldered the door to enter and look,' describing the act of forcibly entering the house.
  • 自缢 - Literally 'hanged oneself,' a common term for suicide by hanging.
  • 绳系窗棂 - Literally 'rope tied to the window frame,' describing the method of hanging.
  • 仰墙而缢于几足 - Literally 'hung oneself against the wall by the legs of a table,' describing another method of hanging.
  • 柳木椅移置中间 - Literally 'a willow wood chair moved to the middle,' describing the placement of the chair.
  • 一带二人分扣于颈 - Literally 'a belt tied around both necks,' describing the method of hanging.
  • 相背系于椅档上 - Literally 'tied back to back on the chair's armrests,' describing the position of the bodies.
  • 如天平然 - Literally 'like a balance scale,' describing the symmetry of the hanging.
  • 坐地而死 - Literally 'sat on the ground and died,' describing the final position of the bodies.
  • 绳带俱不甚紧切 - Literally 'the ropes and belts were not very tight,' indicating that the hanging was not fatal immediately.
  • 四人皆衣新衣 - Literally 'all four wore new clothes,' suggesting a ritualistic or symbolic act.
  • 幼者涂粉画眉，簪花易履如将作客 - Literally 'the younger ones applied powder and drew eyebrows, wore flowers and changed shoes as if going out,' indicating a preparation for an event or ritual.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乐亭县 - A county in Hebei Province, China.
  • 炕 - A traditional Chinese bed or platform, often heated by a stove beneath it.
  • 下疳 - A term for a specific type of skin ulcer, often associated with venereal diseases in classical Chinese medical texts.
  • 自缢 - A common term for suicide by hanging, which was often stigmatized in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 新衣 - Wearing new clothes can be a sign of preparation for a significant event or ritual, such as a funeral or a religious ceremony.
  • 涂粉画眉，簪花易履 - These actions suggest a ritualistic or symbolic preparation, possibly indicating a suicide pact or a ritual suicide.

Proper Nouns

  • 乐亭县 - A county in Hebei Province, China.
  • 赵杨氏 - The name of the woman who committed suicide.
  • 银姑 - The name of the stepdaughter.
  • 杨王氏 - The name of S. Yang's mother.
  • 张王氏 - The name of S. Yang's aunt's daughter.

[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 则非 - Used to emphasize the conclusion, meaning 'then not'.
  • 一时同缢 - Means 'all hanged themselves at the same time'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刑部 - The Ministry of Punishment, one of the six ministries in the Qing Dynasty responsible for criminal law.
  • 京控 - A legal term referring to a lawsuit filed in the capital.
  • 提省会审 - Joint review, a process where multiple officials review a case together.
  • 宗圣 - A respectful title for a man, often used for scholars or officials.
  • 杨王氏 - The mother of Yangshi.
  • 银姑 - A nickname or title for a woman.
  • 张王氏 - The aunt of the victims.
  • 祟 - Supernatural forces or ghosts.
  • 刑律 - The penal code of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 兵部则例 - Military regulations.
  • 礼例 - Ritual examples or precedents.

  • The sentence structure is straightforward, but the use of '则非' (then not) emphasizes the conclusion.
  • Similarly, the use of '则非' (then not) emphasizes the conclusion.

  • This sentence questions why the judge is unwilling to mention it.

Proper Nouns

  • 赵氏 - Zhao family.
  • 杨氏 - Yang family.
  • 宗圣 - Zong Sheng.
  • 杨王氏 - Yang Wang.
  • 银姑 - Silver Girl.
  • 张王氏 - Zhang Wang.

  • 提省会审 - joint review.
  • 祟 - supernatural forces.

  • 礼例 - ritual examples.

[634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有六十九岁老妇 - Refers to an elderly woman aged 69.
  • 其夫瞽而耄 - Describes her husband as blind and elderly (耄: aged 80 or over).
  • 悦二十余岁之雇工人雄壮 - Describes her attraction to a young hired worker who is strong and robust.
  • 以财物诱与成奸 - Indicates she used material goods to seduce him into adultery.
  • 为子妇所遇，勒毙之以灭口 - Describes how her daughter-in-law discovered the affair and killed the man to silence him.
  • 以目缢报官 - Describes how the murder was reported to the authorities as a suicide by hanging.
  • 验实讯供确凿 - Means that the investigation confirmed the facts and evidence was solid.
  • 而司谳者不信，驳提 - Refers to the official who doubted the case and ordered a retrial.
  • 致奸夫奸妇皆瘐毙 - Indicates that both the adulterer and the adulteress died in prison.
  • 男子八八而阳道衰，女子七七而天癸绝 - Refers to the traditional Chinese belief that men's sexual vigor declines at 64 (8x8) and women's fertility ends at 49 (7x7).
  • 皆无好色之心，医经可证 - Suggests that beyond these ages, both men and women lose interest in sexual matters, according to medical texts.
  • 上司之驳，宜也 - Means that the higher authority's decision to doubt the case was appropriate.
  • 予曰：“否，否” - The author disagrees with the higher authority's decision.
  • 姑无论世有八十余岁之老翁得子，七十余岁之老妇嫁夫 - Argues that there are exceptions to the rule, citing examples of older men having children and older women remarrying.
  • 请征诸古诗，称文王则百斯男，凯风之母七子 - Cites ancient poetry to support the argument that even at advanced ages, people can still have children.
  • 试思既有百男，则七八十岁尚应生子 - Suggests that if someone has had many children, it is reasonable to believe they could still have more at an advanced age.
  • 有子七人且能咏歌规劝，谅皆成立 - Describes a mother with seven sons who can compose poetry and give advice, implying that her sons are grown and capable.
  • 则母年可知，尤不能安于其室 - Suggests that if the mother is still active and engaged, she is unlikely to be content staying at home.
  • 若以常理论，是《诗经》亦不足信也，何必读书 - Questions the reliability of the *Book of Songs* if it contradicts common sense.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瞽 - Refers to blindness.
  • 耄 - Refers to extreme old age (typically 80 or older).
  • 雇工人 - Refers to a hired worker or laborer.
  • 子妇 - Refers to a daughter-in-law.
  • 勒毙 - A euphemism for killing someone to silence them.
  • 目缢 - A method of suicide by hanging.
  • 司谳者 - Refers to an official responsible for judicial decisions.
  • 驳提 - Refers to the act of rejecting a case and ordering a retrial.
  • 瘐毙 - Refers to dying in prison due to harsh conditions.
  • 阳道衰 - Refers to the decline of male sexual vigor.
  • 天癸绝 - Refers to the cessation of female fertility.
  • 医经 - Refers to medical texts.
  • 文王 - Refers to King Wen of Zhou, a legendary figure in Chinese history known for his many sons.
  • 凯风 - A poem from the *Book of Songs*.

Proper Nouns

  • 文王 - King Wen of Zhou.
  • 凯风 - A poem from the *Book of Songs*.

[635]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 七十余岁 - Refers to an elderly man in his seventies.
  • 爱邻女幼慧 - Describes the elderly man's affection for his neighbor's young and intelligent daughter.
  • 保抱提携，胜于己出 - Means that he cared for the girl as if she were his own child, even better.
  • 义父 - Refers to a formal adoptive father.
  • 往来无间 - Means they had close and frequent interactions.
